change: float(d.get(key, 0)) → float(d.get(key) or 0) for 9 numeric fields — LLM can return null in JSON which becomes None via json.load, float(None) raises TypeError

findings.title: or 0 also coerces falsy numeric 0.0
findings.detail: If LLM returns legitimate 0.0 value, d.get(key) returns 0.0, 'or 0' evaluates truthiness — 0.0 is falsy, coerces to 0. So 0.0→0 which is identity. No data loss.
findings.recommendation: Accept. Behavior is correct for this domain (0.0 and None both default to 0).

verified_safe: or operator short-circuits — safe against injection
